sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id BIGINT, value DOUBLE, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, 123.456, 'Row One'), (2, NULL, 'Row Two'), (3, 789.01, 'Row Three'), (4, 456.789, 'Row Four');
CREATE TABLE another_data (key_id BIGINT, amount DOUBLE, notes TEXT);
INSERT INTO another_data VALUES (1, 3.14, 'Note A'), (2, 2.718, 'Note B'), (3, NULL, 'Note C');
SELECT id, value, rank() OVER (PARTITION BY description ORDER BY value << 2) AS ranking FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;

SELECT ad.key_id, ad.amount << 1 AS shifted_amount, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count FROM another_data ad INNER JOIN test_data td ON ad.key_id = td.id GROUP BY ad.key_id, ad.amount HAVING shifted_amount >= 10;
SELECT td.id, ((rank() OVER (ORDER BY td.value)) << 1) AS rank_shifted FROM test_data td WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM another_data ad WHERE ad.key_id = td.id AND ad.amount IS NOT NULL);
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT (rank() OVER (PARTITION BY description ORDER BY value))) AS unique_rankings, (NULL << 5) AS null_shifted FROM test_data GROUP BY description HAVING unique_rankings > 1;
SELECT MIN(value << 1) AS min_shifted_value FROM test_data WHERE description NOT LIKE '%Two%';
SELECT rank() OVER (ORDER BY value IS NULL) AS rank_nulls FROM test_data;
SELECT value, rank() OVER (ORDER BY value) AS rank_value, (rank() OVER (ORDER BY value) << 1) AS rank_double FROM test_data ORDER BY value DESC;
